description: The RS200 is Bajaj's fully-faired sport bike. Full fairing, projector headlamp, and a refined engine make it stand out from the naked NS200. It is not a track weapon, but it is a sporty commuter that looks the part and performs well on Indian roads.
pros: Full fairing provides good wind protection
Projector headlamp is excellent at night
cons: Weight makes it less agile than NS200
Pillion seat is small and uncomfortable
Service costs are higher than naked Pulsars

common_problems.solution: Clean and lubricate chain every 500 km

common_problems.cause: Stock seat not designed for long rides
common_problems.solution: Add seat cushion or upgrade seat

buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories fit perfectly but cost more. For the Pulsar RS200, aftermarket offers good value for non-critical items like phone mounts and bike covers. For crash protection, go OEM or from a trusted brand.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
